question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: '] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusTimestamp is a nanosecond-precision Long to perform a binary search attack — halving the timestamp search space with existsById() calls — to locate any specific contract execution in O(log N) queries?
